title: ESCALATE: SIGTERM does not shut down HTTP server

text: {"type":"escalate","finding":"SIGTERM does not shut down HTTP server","severity":"important","description":"Custom _handle_sigterm signal handler replaces Python's default SIGTERM handler (which raises SystemExit). Handler sets self._shutdown and self._wake, but bottle.run() -> serve_forever() blocks main thread and does not check these events. Process becomes unkillable via kill (SIGTERM). Only kill -9 works. Installing the custom handler actually makes this WORSE than having no handler at all.","options":[{"label":"Option 1: raise SystemExit(0) in handler","solution":"Replace flag-setting in _handle_sigterm with raise SystemExit(0). serve_forever() propagates SystemExit. Poll thread is daemon so it dies with process.","pros":"Most Pythonic. Lets Python runtime handle cleanup (atexit, file flushes). serve_forever() is designed to handle this.","cons":"Behavior depends on Bottle/WSGIRef internals propagating the exception correctly."},{"label":"Option 2: os._exit(0) after setting shutdown events","solution":"After setting shutdown event, call os._exit(0) for immediate process exit.","pros":"Guaranteed exit. No dependency on framework internals.","cons":"Skips all cleanup (atexit handlers, file flushes, thread join). Brutal."},{"label":"Option 3: Keep current behavior, document limitation","solution":"Document that systemd TimeoutStopSec / launchd ExitTimeOut handle SIGKILL after timeout.","pros":"No code change. Systemd/launchd handle it operationally.","cons":"Server is unkillable via normal kill for 30s+ timeout. Poor operational behavior."}],"recommendation":"Option 1 (raise SystemExit(0)). Poll thread is daemon, no files to flush, only state is in-memory cache."}
{"type":"user-decision","approach":"Replace flag-setting in _handle_sigterm with raise SystemExit(0). serve_forever() propagates SystemExit. Poll thread is daemon so it dies with the process."}
